purpose: Reinforce ×10 with reduced scaffolding; build toward automaticity.
visual: Equation Builder: `6 × 10 = [___]`. Factors pre-filled. Methods C/D active. Tile palette: 10, 16, 30, 50, 60, 70.
guide: "More 10s. 6 times 10. Skip-count by 10s and place the product."
prompt: "Find the product of 6 × 10. Place the tile."
student_action: Equation Builder Methods C or D (product tile only)
on_correct: "6 times 10 equals 60. Ends in zero so it shows the 10s pattern."
remediation_note: Student counts by 10s six times; use "ends in zero" as self-check for ×10 pattern.
design_note: Reinforces ×10 with reduced scaffolding. Guide identifies the family and references the pattern but doesn't narrate the full skip-count. Student computes independently. Combined with 3.1 (discovery + full build) and 3.2 (graph callback), this gives ×10 three computational touches in Section 3 alone — addressing the audit's ×10 coverage gap. The fourth ×10 touch comes in EC.3.
